issue_title: Missing switch "ID Token as detached signature" in the admin console client settings
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the previous KEycloak versions (EG. Keycloak 18 matching to RH-SSO 7.6) in the old admin console there was a switch `Use ID Token as a Detached Signature` with the tooltip `This makes ID token returned from Authorization Endpoint in OIDC Hybrid flow use as a detached signature defined in FAPI 1.0 Advanced Security Profile. Therefore, this ID token does not include an authenticated user's information.`

The switch was within the "Advanced Settings" for OIDC Clients.

Looks like we forgot to add this switch to the new admin console. The switch was probably added to the old admin console around the time when the particular screen was migrated to the new admin console and hence it ended to never being added to the new admin console. We had similar issues before as well for some other switches.

### Version

nightly (from 2025-09-19), 26.2, 26.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Switch being available in the admin console

### Actual behavior

Switch not being available in the admin console

### How to Reproduce?

See details

### Anything else?

There is a workaround to use directly admin REST API (or maybe kcadm). The name of related attribute is `id.token.as.detached.signature` .

Missing switch 'ID Token as detached signature' in the admin console client settings
closes #42769

issue_title: [OID4VCI] Adjust authorization_details generation and credential identifier mapping for OIDVCI Conformace Test
issue_body:
### Description

OID4VCI Conformance Tests were failing because the system wasn't generating `authorization_details` in token responses when not provided in token requests.

According to the OID4VCI specification (Section 6.2), `authorization_details` is:
- **REQUIRED** when the `authorization_details` parameter is used in the token request
- **OPTIONAL** when the `scope` parameter was used instead

In pre-authorized code flows, since there's no use of the authorization endpoint, the only way to request with authorization details is in the token request. However, conformance tests send a token request **without** authorization details and expect to receive them in the response.

## Solution
- Generate `authorization_details` from the credential offer's `credential_configuration_ids` when not provided in the token request
- Include the generated authorization details in the token response so conformance tests pass
- Fix credential identifier mapping to ensure credential requests work correctly with the generated identifiers
commit_message:
[OID4VCI] Fix authorization_details generation and credential identifier mapping for conformance tests (#42819)

Closes: #42818

Signed-off-by: forkimenjeckayang <forkimenjeckayang@gmail.com>

issue_title: Organization selection changes after token refresh
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

If two or more concurrent tokens are active for the same user/client session (ie: OIDC across two tabs in the same browser for the same client), the selected organization will switch to the most recently authenticated as soon as the refresh token is used to generate a new access token.

Relates to #35935.
- the fix enabled two concurrent auth sessions to track different orgs for the same user/client session, however this does not persist across refresh of the access token.

In practice, the symptom looks like this.
- A user of an SPA authenticates in a browser - selecting orgA.   Starts interacting with the application for tenant A.
- User opens a new tab, upon reauthentication selects orgB.   Starts interacting with the application for tenant B.
- After 5 min (default access token expiry), the first tab will refresh its token (silent refresh).  The application is now in an inconsistent state where the selection has suddenly changed to tenant B.

### Version

26.3.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Refreshing an access token should retain the same organization selection that existed before the refresh

The mapper should respect the requested scope (assuming the org still exists, following "Requires User Membership" behavior)
- for `grant_type: refresh_token`, 
  - if the optional `scope` isn't provided, then use the scope from the `refresh_token`
  - if the optional `scope` is provided, use the requested scope instead `organization:XXX`.  the scope of the new tokens should be consistent.

### Actual behavior

Refreshing an access token will switch the organization selection to the most recently selected for the client/user session.    
* The mapper pulls the selection from the clientSession note.

### How to Reproduce?

Having a realm with organizations enabled
- create organization foo and bar 
- create a public client
- add a realm user w/ credentials
- add realm user to both orgs foo and bar

Retrieve two concurrent access tokens for the realm user `protocol/openid-connect/auth`
- only scope differs between the two
1) `openid organization:foo`
2) `openid organization:bar`

Both sets of access token/refresh token will contain both the respective requested scope and org selection
== GOOD

Refresh the token authenticated for foo (1) - `protocol/openid-connect/token` with `grant_type: refresh_token`
- Inspect the access token - scope is retained `organization:foo`, but selected org SWITCHED to bar
```
  "scope": "openid organization:foo",
  "organization": {
    "bar": {
      "id": "719193d1-08d5-42ad-838c-abbf8ddede4f"
    }
  },
``` 
== BAD





### Anything else?

While the repro steps involve using the `organization:xxx` scope to select the organization, using the `organization` scope to prompt organization selection via the browser flow exhibits the same unexpected behavior.

In this scenario, the requested scope cannot be used to infer the selection within the `refresh_token` as it will be simply `organization`.
- one possibility, though not sure this is possible from a mapper, is to rewrite the organization scope `organization` to be the more specific selected scope `organization:foo`
- another possibility might be to persist a new claim to hold the selection in the refresh token and always use this instead

summarizing,
- regardless of  whether the org selection was made directly via scope or during the browser flow, the accessToken and its refreshes (via refreshToken) should persist this selection (assuming it stays valid)

issue_title: LOGIN event without a user session
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When a user authenticates for the first time to the client, the LOGIN event is generated without the user session.
The problem is in the consent approval flow.
In the [LoginActionsService](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/LoginActionsService.java#L1073), the event is sent before the session is created:

```java
        event.detail(Details.CONSENT, Details.CONSENT_VALUE_CONSENT_GRANTED);
        event.success();

        ClientSessionContext clientSessionCtx = AuthenticationProcessor.attachSession(authSession, null, session, realm, clientConnection, event);
        return AuthenticationManager.redirectAfterSuccessfulFlow(session, realm, clientSessionCtx.getClientSession().getUserSession(), clientSessionCtx, request, session.getContext().getUri(), clientConnection, event, authSession);
```

### Version

26.3.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The LOGIN event contains the user session when consent is approved.

### Actual behavior

The LOGIN event doesn't contain the user session when consent is approved.

### How to Reproduce?

Log in to the client with consent for the first time.

### Anything else?

The PR that will fix the issue is here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/42866

User session in LOGIN event (#42866)

closes #42867

issue_title: Valid scope parameter in access token request is rejected with invalid_scope error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

A valid OIDC/OAuth2 token request is rejected with error code `invalid_scope` in case the token request contains a valid scope parameter which includes at least 2 scopes which use dynamic scope format.

It seems this regression was introduced with the commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/8f9d02c3057205129e08872943505829286500d7 related to pull request https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/39752 and issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/39402.

The issue is also present if the experimental feature `DYNAMIC_SCOPES` is disabled. The code introduced in the PR above is not guarded by a feature enabled check.

### Version

26.3.4

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A valid OIDC/OAuth2 token request should not be rejected with error.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak responds with HTTP 400 and error code `invalid_scope`.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Keep experimental Keycloak feature `DYNAMIC_SCOPES` disabled.
2. Create two scopes where the second scope has the name of the first scope as prefix and having a `:` separator and any suffix. For example `scope1` and `scope1:A`
3. Create a client (for example `client_1` with client_credentials flow enabled) and configure both previously created scopes as **default** scopes.
4. Make a simple token request which explicitly includes both scopes in the token request parameter. For example with a simple curl HTTP request.

`curl -X POST --location "https://<keycloak_host>/realms/<realm>/protocol/openid-connect/token" -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" -d 'grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=test_client&client_secret=<client_secret>&scope=scope1 scope1:A'`

The response from Keycloak is
`{"error":"invalid_scope","error_description":"Invalid scopes: scope1 scope1:A"}`

If you only request a single scope or do not include any scope parameter at all everything is working as expected because the problematic code only kicks in if there are multiple scopes with a specific format. See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/TokenManager.java#L643 as reference.

issue_title: Race condition in authorization service leads to NullPointerException when evaluating permissions during concurrent resource deletion
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

When running Keycloak in a clustered environment with multiple pods, concurrent deletion of an Authorization Resource on one pod would trigger a NullPointerException in another pod that is simultaneously evaluating permissions for the same resource.

It seems like this is caused by a race condition between resource deletion, cache invalidation, and permission evaluation

Impact: Users experience intermittent login failures (500 errors) when resource deletion overlaps with client login/permission evaluation. This issue is especially visible in multi-pod deployments with concurrent resource management operations.

### Version

26.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Permission evaluation should handle concurrent deletion gracefully. If a resource no longer exists, Keycloak should either: 1) Skip evaluation of that resource, or 2) Fail with a controlled error (not an uncaught NPE).

### Actual behavior

The pod evaluating permissions throws a NullPointerException:

```
Unexpected error while evaluating permissions: java.lang.RuntimeException: Failed to evaluate permissions
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.authorization.model.Resource.getScopes()" because "resource" is null
```

This corresponds to a 500 Internal Server Error response during login.

### How to Reproduce?

Steps to Reproduce

1. Run Keycloak with at least 2 pods behind a load balancer, sharing the same database.
2. Create a client with fine-grained Authorization enabled and some resources with policies.
3. Trigger a login flow for the client (which causes AuthorizationTokenService to evaluate permissions).
4. At the same time, issue a DELETE request for one of the Authorization Resources in that client.
5. Example: DELETE /{realm}/clients/{client-uuid}/authz/resource-server/resource/{resource-uuid}
6. Observe the logs on the pod handling the login flow.

### Anything else?

Keycloak version: 26.0.7

Deployment: Kubernetes, multiple pods (stateless)

Filter invalid resources and scopes when processing entries from the cache

Closes #42907

issue_title: Make sure TestEventsListenerProvider does not reuse the events in memory
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

We detected in this PR https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/42866 that the providers we are using for testing events are just adding the event passed to a list. This way it can happen that the event is passed not fully filled but later is completed with the missing info. As the list is created with the same event instance, the event is returned OK for the test although it was incorrect at the time the method was called. This means that another provider (for example one that sends the event serialized via network) would not be executed OK and failed because some info was not set.

This issue is to change the test providers to `clone` the event with the current information at calling time. We have to check if there are errors in the CI after the change and fix the event generation for them.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The event should be stored in the test provider with the data sent at calling time. The object sshould be duplicated/cloned to avoid this issue.

### Actual behavior

The event is just added to the list, so the instance is the same and can trigger this nasty issue.

### How to Reproduce?

See description and issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/42867.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure events are fully filled before success is called
Closes #42914

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Incorrect Basic Auth encoding for OIDC IDentity Provider when Client ID contains colon
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Based on [OAuth2.1 Client Secret](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#name-client-secret), when an Identity Provider has `Client authentication` as `Client secret sent as basic auth` basic authentication encoding must be `: based64_encode(url_encode(client_id) + ":" + url_encode(client_secret))`.

Keycloak need to respect it and send the basic authenticator encoding based on OAuth 2.1

### Version

26.3.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Basic authentication encoding is `: based64_encode(url_encode(client_id) + ":" + url_encode(client_secret))`.

### Actual behavior

Basic authentication encoding is `: based64_encode(client_id + ":" +client_secret)`.

### How to Reproduce?

Create an OIDC Identity Provider with Basic Authenticator and client_id including colon (fe https://www.keycloak.org/). Observe the basic authentication that Keycloak sends.
When you will try to login, authentication will be failed due to wrong client credentials.
Keycloak client will try to authenticate with client_id = 'https'

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Saving Client “Advanced” sets Request Object signature/encryption attributes to “any” even when not changed
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In Keycloak 26.3.5, saving the _Client > Settings > Advanced_ page without changing the **Request Object**-related selects results in the following client attributes being persisted with the literal value “any”:

- request.object.signature.alg
- request.object.encryption.alg
- request.object.encryption.enc

If the user hasn’t changed these selects, the attributes should remain unspecified (null) rather than being stored as “any”. Persisting “any” leads to internal validation issues when using PAR + JAR (Pushed Authorization Requests with JWT-secured Authorization Request).


**Impact**
PAR + JAR flows can fail due to algorithm validation, as “any” is treated as an explicit client-selected value, leading to mismatches between the configured algorithm and the JWT header.
Relevant Errors/Logs During request object validation, when the attribute is “any”, the algorithm comparison may raise errors such as:
“Request object signed with different algorithm than client requested algorithm” (see org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.request.AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser#createRequestObjectValidator)

### Version

26.3.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The attributes remain unspecified (null) when the user does not change these fields in the UI, and neither the Admin Console nor the Admin REST API persist the literal value “any”

### Actual behavior

After saving, the three attributes are stored as “any” like this:
`
{
    "id": "ca974775-50f6-4c27-b6f1-fd4109b18c88",
    "clientId": "par-jar-client",
    ...
    "attributes": {
        "request.object.signature.alg": "any",
        "request.object.encryption.enc": "any",
        "request.object.encryption.alg": "any",
    ...
`

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create or open an OIDC client.
2. Go to Client > Settings > Advanced.
3. Do not change the following selects:
    1. Request Object Signature Algorithm
    2. Request Object Encryption Algorithm (alg)
    3. Request Object Content Encryption Algorithm (enc) 
4. Click “Save”.
5. Inspect the client attributes in the Admin Console or via the Admin REST API or export realm with clients details

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Duplicate Email Fields on Temporarily Locked Out Sign In With Organization Identity-First Login
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area login/ui ### Describe the bug When an account is temporarily locked on a realm with Organization Identity-First Login execution, attempting to login results in a form with two email fields. The first field is disabled with a "Restart Login" button; the second field allows input. I would speculate that the second field should not be there at all. Regardless of if the user attempts to sign into their account with the correct password or a different account altogether, login fails, so there is no reason to have an email that takes input on this form. ### Version 26.4 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Given the behavior of the keycloak login form as it is now, I would expect one disabled email field with a "restart login" button. If the user decided for whatever reason that they had the wrong username/email, they would need to utilize the restart login button to proceed with their attempts to log into another account. ### Actual behavior The user is taken to a form with two email fields, one disabled and one that takes input. The field that takes input is a bit misleading, however, because the user will be unable to successfully login from that form. Hypothetically, If the user typos their way to this double-email form, they will see the following: The presence of an email field that takes input and a password field will imply to the user that they will be able to login with their correct credentials from this point, but that is not the case; they will instead be met with an "Invalid username or password" validation message. ### How to Reproduce? 1) In Authentication Settings, bind a browser flow with Organization Identity-First Login execution. 2)) In Realm Settings -> Security Defenses, enable Lockout Temporarily Brute Force Detection (and lower max login failures from default 1000) 3) Trigger temporary lockout and attempt to login ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: Regression in DEBUG_PORT handling since 26.4.0 – host binding (*:port / 0.0.0.0:port) no longer works
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Since Keycloak 26.4.0 (after #39924), running Keycloak in Docker with remote debugging enabled fails when using host binding in `DEBUG_PORT` (e.g., `*:8787` or `*:5005`).

In 26.3.5, the same setup worked as expected.

### Version

26.4.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should start successfully with `DEBUG_PORT="*:5005"` and the debugger should be listening on the specified port.

### Actual behavior

In 26.4.0, Keycloak fails to start with the following error when using host binding in `DEBUG_PORT`:
```
ERROR: transport error 202: getaddrinfo: failed to parse address
ERROR: JDWP Transport dt_socket failed to initialize, TRANSPORT_INIT(510)
JDWP exit error AGENT_ERROR_TRANSPORT_INIT(197): No transports initialized [src/jdk.jdwp.agent/share/native/libjdwp/debugInit.c:700]
```

✅ Works with `DEBUG_PORT=5005` (just the port number, no host binding).
✅ Works with only `DEBUG: "true"` (no DEBUG_PORT), which binds to the default port 8787.
❌ Fails with DEBUG_PORT="*:5005".

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a docker-compose.yml using Keycloak 26.4.0 with debugging enabled:

```yml
services:
  keycloak-26-4-0:
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.4.0
    container_name: keycloak-26-4-0
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
      - "5005:5005"
    environment:
      KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME: "admin"
      KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD: "admin"
      KC_HTTP_PORT: "8080"
      DEBUG: "true"
      DEBUG_PORT: "*:5005"
    command: ["start-dev"]
```

2. Start the container:

`docker compose up`

3. Observe the startup failure with JDWP transport errors.

Repeat with Keycloak 26.3.5 using the same setup — the container starts successfully and the debugger listens on port 5005.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Backend url misses the path with reverse proxy
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/api ### Describe the bug The Backend URL is not calculated correctly behind a reverse proxy. The path is missing. This leads to the situation that our frontend is trying to get a token from the token URL, but querries the base URL ### Version 26.4.0 (docker: keycloak/keycloak:latest) ### Regression - [x] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior The Backend URL matches the base URL (Proto+Host+Path) of the Request URL Since I am using xForward proxy headers I would expect: X-Forwarded-Proto + X-Forwarded-Host + X-Forwarded-Prefix ### Actual behavior The Backend URL misses the base path if I acces the server directly like a backend service would everyting is fine ### How to Reproduce? here is my base example: run: docker compose up -d --build .env ``` .env SERVER_NAME=localhost DB_PASSWORD=pUIqsDsRUMnq3Lh8 ``` certs.sh ``` bash #!/bin/bash -e if [ ! -e "/etc/nginx/ssl/cert.pem" ]; then echo "Create certs" openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout /etc/nginx/ssl/key.pem -out /etc/nginx/ssl/cert.pem -sha256 -days 3650 -nodes -subj "/CN=$ENV_SERVER_NAME" else echo "Certs allready created" fi ``` docker-compose.yaml ``` yaml version: '3' volumes: pgdata: logs: plugins: certs: services: proxy: image: local_proxy build: . ports: - '80:80' - '443:443' environment: ENV_SERVER_NAME: ${SERVER_NAME} volumes: - type: volume source: certs target: /etc/nginx/ssl read_only: false depends_on: - keycloak keycloak: image: keycloak/keycloak command: start ports: - '8090:8080' environment: KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME: 'admin' KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD: 'admin' KC_DB: 'postgres' KC_DB_URL: 'jdbc:postgresql://database:5432/keycloak' KC_DB_USERNAME: postgres KC_DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD} KC_HOSTNAME: "https://${SERVER_NAME}/keycloak" KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT: false KC_HOSTNAME_DEBUG: true KC_HTTP_ENABLED: true KC_PROXY_HEADERS: xforwarded KC_HOSTNAME_BACKCHANNEL_DYNAMIC: true depends_on: - database database: image: 'postgres:14' ports: - '5430:5432' volumes: - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD} POSTGRES_DB: 'keycloak' healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER}"] interval: 10s timeout: 20s retries: 5 ``` Dockerfile ``` Dockerfile FROM nginx:alpine # Add bash RUN apk add --no-cache bash RUN apk add --no-cache openssl RUN touch /etc/nginx/conf.d/proxy.conf RUN rm /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf COPY ./certs.sh /etc/nginx/ RUN mkdir /etc/nginx/ssl WORKDIR /etc/nginx/ RUN dos2unix < certs.sh > certs2.sh COPY ./proxy.conf.template /etc/nginx/ # Default port exposure EXPOSE 80 EXPOSE 443 # CMD ["tail", "-f", "/dev/null"] CMD ["sh", "-c", "sh certs2.sh && envsubst '${ENV_SERVER_NAME}' < proxy.conf.template > conf.d/proxy.conf && nginx-debug -g \"daemon off;\""] ``` proxy.conf.template ``` conf server { listen 80; server_name _; return 301 https://$ENV_SERVER_NAME$request_uri; } server { listen 443 ssl; server_name _; client_max_body_size 0; ssl_certificate /etc/nginx/ssl/cert.pem; ssl_certificate_key /etc/nginx/ssl/key.pem; location /keycloak/ { rewrite ^/keycloak/(.*)$ /$1 break; proxy_pass http://keycloak:8080; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Scheme $scheme; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Prefix /keycloak; proxy_set_header X-Original-Forwarded-For $http_x_forwarded_for; } } ``` ### Anything else? We currently are running this setup with keycloak 24, but since 25 its broken

issue_title: [OID4VC] Ensure authorization_details from PAR requests are properly returned in token responses for conformace tests
issue_body: ### Description #### Problem - When `authorization_details` is passed in a PAR request, the system was not properly ensuring it gets returned in the token response - The OID4VC specification requirement for mandatory `authorization_details` in token responses when used in authorization requests was not being enforced #### Solution 1. **Extended AuthorizationDetailsProcessor SPI**: Added `processStoredAuthorizationDetails()` method to handle `authorization_details` from stored authorization requests (PAR) 2. **Enhanced OID4VC Processor**: Updated `OID4VCAuthorizationDetailsProcessor` to process stored `authorization_details` and throw `OAuthErrorException` when processing fails 3. **Improved Error Handling**: Modified `OAuth2GrantTypeBase` to catch `OAuthErrorException` and wrap it in `CorsErrorResponseException` for proper HTTP 400 responses 4. **Updated Authorization Code Flow**: Enhanced `AuthorizationCodeGrantType` to process stored `authorization_details` with proper error handling 5. **Comprehensive Testing**: Added dedicated test suite covering success, failure, and no-details scenarios #### Authorization Code flow with no `authorization_details` #### Authorization Code flow with `authorization_details`

issue_title: Cannot revoke access token generated by Standard Token Exchange
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Access tokens obtained via internal-to-internal Standard Token Exchange cannot be revoked via the TokenRevocationEndpoint. 

### Version

26.4.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It should be possible to use the token revocation endpoint to mark an access token as revoked, regardless if it was created via token-exchange or not.

### Actual behavior

The TokenRevocationEndpoint call fails in the org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenRevocationEndpoint#checkUser method, because 
it fails with "Invalid Token" and the `USER_SESSION_NOT_FOUND` error. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/TokenRevocationEndpoint.java#L221

### How to Reproduce?

1) Setup token exchange with an initial / requester / target client
2) Obtain a token AT1 for the initial client
2) Perform an internal-2-internal tokenchange with AT2 via the requester client with the target client as audience to obtain token AT2
3) Call the token revocation endpoint with AT2 as the requester client

This last step will fail.

### Anything else?

Note that calling the Token introspection endpoint with AT2 from above returns the token claims AND `"active":true`.

Seems to be related to this issue  https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/42565
commit_message:
Use UserSessionUtil.findValidSessionForAccessToken in revocation endpoint
Closes #43218

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>
